englishRequirements.waiverNotes: UC Davis requires English proficiency for international students who studied in the United States for fewer than three years and learned in a language other than English before arriving. Accepted pathways include TOEFL iBT, IELTS, Duolingo, ACT English, SAT Evidence-Based Reading and Writing, approved-country schooling, or two years of continuous full-time study at an approved college or university. Score reports are reviewed for a period of two years from the enrollment term.

scholarshipNotes: UC Davis offers campus-based scholarships through the UC Application for Admission and Scholarships, including merit-based awards. The entering-student scholarship application is due by December 2, and international students may be eligible for scholarships, private loans, and short-term loans even though most other aid types are not available.

testingRequirements.latestSatTestDateNote: If tests are used for minimum subject requirements or course placement, UC Davis says to take them no later than December of the last year before graduation.
testingRequirements.latestActTestDateNote: If tests are used for minimum subject requirements or course placement, UC Davis says to take them no later than December of the last year before graduation.

testPolicy.excerpt: UC Davis no longer considers SAT or ACT test scores for admissions decisions or scholarship awards.

warnings: UC Davis uses a test-blind admissions policy, so testingRequirements is intentionally sparse and the SAT/ACT fields are present only as optional context.
The published 2026-27 cost page includes both resident and nonresident budgets; this artifact uses the nonresident budget for a single publishable cost figure.
